data.title: OMEGA HEALTHCARE INVESTORS INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Omega Healthcare Investors Inc., a leading real estate investment trust (REIT) focused on the healthcare sector, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial growth and strategic operational developments, underscoring the company's robust performance and commitment to expanding its portfolio of high-quality healthcare facilities.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $282.5 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, reflecting an increase from $252.7 million in the same period in 2024, driven by higher rental and interest income.
  • Net Income: $140.5 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $117.1 million in the same period in 2024, indicating improved profitability.
  • Net Income Available to Common Stockholders: $136.6 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, up from $113.9 million in the same period in 2024.
  • Basic EPS: $0.46 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, consistent with the same period in 2024.
  • Diluted EPS: $0.46 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $0.45 in the same period in 2024, showing a slight increase.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: Omega Healthcare Investors, Inc. reported rental income primarily from fixed income operating leases, which accounted for $235.6 million in the three months ended June 30, 2025, and $463.8 million for the six months ended June 30, 2025. Variable income from operating leases, which includes reimbursements for real estate taxes, contributed $3.6 million and $7.4 million for the three and six months ended June 30, 2025, respectively.
  • Geographical Performance: The United Kingdom represented a significant portion of Omega's real estate assets, accounting for 17.8% of the total as of June 30, 2025. This highlights the company's strategic focus on international markets, particularly in the U.K. and Jersey.
  • Sales Units: During the six months ended June 30, 2025, Omega sold 34 facilities, including 32 skilled nursing facilities (SNFs) and two assisted living facilities (ALFs), generating $183.0 million in net cash proceeds. This reflects the company's ongoing strategy to optimize its portfolio by divesting non-core assets.
  • New Product Launches: Omega completed and placed into service the $201.8 million Inspir Embassy Row project, an assisted living facility in Washington D.C., during the first quarter of 2025. This facility is under a 24-year lease agreement, indicating a long-term commitment to expanding its portfolio of high-quality healthcare facilities.
  • New Production Launches: Omega invested $62.7 million in construction in progress and capital improvements during the six months ended June 30, 2025. This includes the development of new skilled nursing facilities in Virginia, Florida, and Maryland, demonstrating the company's focus on expanding its operational capacity.
  • Future Outlook: Omega continues to focus on strategic acquisitions and developments in the healthcare real estate sector, with a particular emphasis on skilled nursing and assisted living facilities. The company is committed to enhancing its portfolio through targeted investments and divestitures to optimize returns and support long-term growth.

SEC Filing: OMEGA HEALTHCARE INVESTORS INC [ OHI ] - 10-Q - Aug. 01, 2025

data.title: KIMCO REALTY CORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Kimco Realty Corporation, a leading Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT) specializing in high-quality, open-air, grocery-anchored shopping centers and mixed-use properties in the United States, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's robust financial performance and significant business activities during the period.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues from rental properties, net: $520.9 million, an increase of $24.7 million compared to the same period in 2024, primarily due to increased leasing activity and property acquisitions.
  • Net income available to the Company's common shareholders: $155.4 million, an increase of $43.7 million compared to the same period in 2024, reflecting higher revenues and gains on property sales.
  • Diluted per common share: $0.23, up from $0.17 in the same period in 2024, indicating improved profitability per share.
  • Operating expenses: $4,242 million, a slight increase of $16 million compared to the same period in 2024, reflecting higher real estate taxes and maintenance costs.
  • Depreciation and amortization: $156.3 million, an increase of $8.2 million compared to the same period in 2024, due to new properties and redevelopment projects.

Business Highlights

  • Company Overview: Kimco Realty Corporation operates as a Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT) and is a leading owner and operator of high-quality, open-air, grocery-anchored shopping centers and mixed-use properties in the United States.
  • Portfolio Composition: As of June 30, 2025, the company had interests in 566 U.S. shopping center properties, totaling 100.9 million square feet of gross leasable area, and 67 other property interests, primarily net leased properties and preferred equity investments, totaling 5.5 million square feet.
  • RPT Merger: On January 2, 2024, Kimco Realty completed a merger with RPT Realty, adding 56 open-air shopping centers to its portfolio, including 43 wholly owned and 13 joint venture assets, comprising 13.3 million square feet of gross leasable area.
  • Leasing Activity: During the six months ended June 30, 2025, the company executed 854 leases totaling 6.3 million square feet, including 264 new leases and 590 renewals and options, with an average rent per square foot of $23.42 for new leases and $20.37 for renewals.
  • Tenant Concentration: The company's five largest tenants as of June 30, 2025, were The TJX Companies, Ross Stores, Burlington Stores, Inc., Amazon/Whole Foods, and Albertsons Companies, Inc., representing a significant portion of the company's annualized base rental revenues.
  • Same Property NOI: Same property net operating income increased by 3.1% for the three months ended June 30, 2025, and by 3.5% for the six months ended June 30, 2025, primarily due to strong leasing activity and increased minimum rent.
  • Future Outlook: The company aims to continue its growth by increasing the value of its existing portfolio, maintaining strong debt metrics, and expanding in desirable demographic areas with successful retailers, primarily focusing on grocery anchors.

SEC Filing: KIMCO REALTY CORP [ KIM ] - 10-Q - Aug. 01, 2025

data.title: Gold Futures Jump on Weaker-Than-Expected U.S. Economic Data — Market Talk
data.description: Gold futures rise after U.S. economic data came in softer than expected. Futures are up 1.6% at $3,402.30 a troy ounce. Nonfarm Payroll data showed just 73,000 jobs were added in July, below analyst expectations of 100,000. Even more suprising, revisions to May and June's data revised jobs down by a huge 258,000, ING analysts say in a note. This highlights that the jobs market has lost momentum even earlier than thought and will intensify pressure from President Trump for the Federal Reserve to loosen monetary policy, ING says. With tariffs set to eat into household spending and corporate profits, the case for cutting interest rates has been significantly strengthened, ING says. Lower interest rates typically benefit non-interest bearing bullion. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

data.title: Mexico Factory Activity Contracts for 13th Month
data.description:
Mexico’s S&P Global Manufacturing PMI rose to 49.1 in July 2025, compared to 46.3 in June, indicating a thirteenth successive deterioration but the softest contraction since January.

The rise reflects a marginal easing in new orders, which firms attributed to lingering US tariffs and heightened competition from Chinese imports.

Factory output also fell, albeit at the mildest pace since February, while purchasing activity declined for the seventh consecutive month as companies sought to adjust inventories amid persistent cost pressures.

Employment decreased for the fifteenth month running, though at the slowest rate in the current period of job shedding.

Input prices continued to climb at the steepest rate in three months, yet most producers absorbed these higher costs, with selling prices rising only marginally.

Despite the ongoing downturn, sentiment improved to an eight-month high, though growth expectations remain subdued within the context of the historical series.

data.title: Watches of Switzerland Shares Drop on U.S. Tariffs News — Market Talk
data.description: Shares in Watches of Switzerland plunge following the latest news on President Trump's tariffs on Swiss products. The U.S. unveiled plans to impose a 39% tariff rate on goods imports from Switzerland, effective Aug. 7. The new rate is higher than the 31% announced on April 2. The U.K. luxury-watch retailer, which sells brands including Rolex, has a strong presence in the U.S. The country accounts for about 48% of its sales, according to its latest annual report. The stock trades 7.6% lower. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

data.title: Week Ahead - Aug 4th
data.description:
Next week, investor attention will remain focused on President Trump’s trade war, following the announcement of sweeping tariffs on August 1st. Meanwhile, the earnings season continues in full swing, with key reports due from Palantir Technologies, Walt Disney, AMD, Amgen, McDonald’s, and Eli Lilly.

In the US, important economic data releases include the ISM Services PMI, trade balance, factory orders, and the preliminary estimate of Q2 productivity and labor costs.

Globally, monetary policy decisions are expected from the BoE, the RBI, and Mexico’s central bank.

Other key data include trade and inflation for China; retail sales and producer prices for the Eurozone; factory orders, industrial production, and trade figures for Germany; trade and PMIs for Australia; trade and employment for Canada; inflation from Mexico, Switzerland and Turkey; and GDP growth figures from Indonesia and the Philippines.

data.title: REG - Novacyt S.A. - Liquidity Agreement Monthly Update & TVR
data.description:
RNS Number : 6985T Novacyt S.A. 01 August 2025  

Novacyt S.A.

("Novacyt", the "Company" or the "Group")

Liquidity Agreement Monthly Update and Total Voting Rights

Paris, France and Manchester, UK - 1 August 2025 - Novacyt (EURONEXT GROWTH: ALNOV; AIM: NCYT), an international specialist in clinical diagnostics, announces its monthly update in relation to ordinary shares traded under its ongoing liquidity agreement with Invest Securities SA (the "Liquidity Agreement"). The Liquidity Agreement is governed by French law and is further summarised below.

During the period from 1 July to 31 July 2025, Invest Securities purchased 14,521 ordinary shares at a maximum price of €0.49 and a minimum price of €0.46 and sold 8,516 ordinary shares at a maximum price of €0.49 and a minimum price of €0.47 under the Liquidity Agreement. The total number of ordinary shares in the Company, which are held in treasury as at close of business on 31 July 2025, is 110,007.

Total Voting Rights

The total number of ordinary shares in the Company is 70,626,248. This figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company pursuant to Article L. 223-7 of the French Commercial Code and the Company's Articles. The Company is not subject to the disclosure guidance and transparency rules made by the Financial Conduct Authority under Part VI of FSMA.

Contacts

Novacyt SA

https://novacyt.com/investors

Lyn Rees, Chief Executive Officer

 Via Walbrook PR

Steve Gibson, Chief Financial Officer 

SP Angel Corporate Finance LLP (Nominated Adviser and Broker)

+44 (0)7980 541 893 / +44 (0)7584 391 303 /

+44 (0)7407 804 654

About Novacyt Group (www.novacyt.com)

Novacyt is an international molecular diagnostics company providing a broad portfolio of integrated technologies and services, primarily focused on the delivery of genomic medicine. The Company develops, manufactures, and commercialises a range of molecular assays and instrumentation to deliver workflows and services that enable seamless end-to-end solutions from sample to result across multiple sectors including human health, animal health and environmental.

The Company is divided into three business segments:

Clinical

Broad portfolio of human clinical in vitro diagnostic products, workflows and services focused on three therapeutic areas:

·    Reproductive Health: NIPT, Cystic Fibrosis and other rapid aneuploidy tests

·    Precision Medicine: DPYD genotyping assay

·    Infectious Diseases: Winterplex, multiplex winter respiratory PCR panel

Instrumentation

Portfolio of next generation size selection DNA sample preparation platforms and rapid PCR machines, including:

·    Ranger® Technology: automated DNA sample preparation and target enrichment technology

·    genesig q16 and q32 real-time quantitative PCR (qPCR) instruments

Research Use Only 

Range of services for the life sciences industry:

·    Design, manufacture, and supply of high-performance qPCR assays and workflows for use in human health, agriculture, veterinary and environmental, to support global health organisations and the research industry

·    Pharmaceutical research services: whole genome sequencing (WGS) / whole exome sequencing (WES)

Novacyt is headquartered in Le Vésinet in France with offices in the UK (in Manchester), Singapore, the US and Canada and has a commercial presence in over 65 countries. The Company is listed on the London Stock Exchange's AIM market ("NCYT") and on the Paris Stock Exchange Euronext Growth ("ALNOV").

For more information, please refer to the website: www.novacyt.com

Further information on the Liquidity Agreement

On 12 September 2016, the Company and Invest Securities entered into the Liquidity Agreement pursuant to which Invest Securities provides liquidity services in relation to the ordinary shares to the Company. Invest Securities may purchase ordinary shares on behalf of the Company under the agreement, subject to approval from Shareholders as to the price at which ordinary shares can be brought back and the aggregate amount that the Company may provide to Invest Securities to purchase such ordinary shares.

Shareholder approval was granted at the Shareholders' meeting held on 19 June 2025 for the purchase of ordinary shares by Invest Securities under the agreement at a maximum purchase price of €5.00 per share, up to a limit of 10% of the share capital and for a period of 18 months from the date of the approval. Under the agreement, Invest Securities must act completely independently of the Company and the Company must not communicate with the employees of Invest Securities who are responsible for performing the agreement. Invest Securities is paid €10,000 per annum for its services under the liquidity agreement. The agreement has an initial term of two years, with a rolling extension of one year thereafter. The agreement can be terminated by either party at the end of each such period subject to two months' prior notice. The Liquidity Agreement is governed by French law. Ordinary shares purchased by Invest Securities are either cancelled or held as treasury shares (which are non-voting and do not rank for dividends).

This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom.
